params.amount: The slash amount to absorb from the pool

params.validatorAddress: The validator whose pool to claim from

completeUndelegation(address).details: SLASH PROTECTION: Returns the full undelegateAmount without deducting post-exit slashes.      Unbonding tokens were removed from pool.totalDelegated at request time, so      slashPerShareAccum increases during the unbonding period do not affect this amount.

params.validatorAddress: The validator to complete undelegation from

delegate(address,uint256).details: Delegator must approve this contract to spend RLS tokens before callingWhen the whitelist gate is enabled, caller must already be verified.      Use `delegateWithProof` to verify for the first time.

params.amount: The amount of RLS tokens to delegate

delegateWithProof(address,uint256,uint25.details: Verifies the caller against the whitelist Merkle root when the gate is      enabled and the caller has not yet been verified. After a successful      verification the result is cached and subsequent delegations can use      the 2-arg `delegate`. When the gate is disabled or the caller is already      verified, the proof is ignored.

params.amount: The amount of RLS tokens to delegate
params.lockboxBalance: The caller's balance as encoded in the lockbox snapshot leaf

disableWhitelist().details: Admin-only. The root and cached verifications are preserved —      calling `setWhitelistRoot` again re-enables the gate.

distributePoolRewards(address,uint256).details: RewardDistributor must transfer RLS tokens to this contract before calling

params.amount: The amount of RLS tokens to distribute

returns._0: The address that will receive commission when claimed

params.validatorAddress: The validator whose pool to query

returns.effectiveAmount: The delegator's stake after applying pending slashes

returns._0: The pending commission details (newBps=0 if none pending)
The address that will receive rewards when claimed

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

getTotalDelegatedStake(address).details: Intended for Rust consensus to read for future weighted voting power

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

requestUndelegation(address,uint256).details: The unbonding period is locked at request time and not affected by future config changesSLASH PROTECTION: The undelegation amount is removed from pool.totalDelegated immediately,      so any future slashes (via applyPoolSlash) only affect remaining active delegators.      The unbonding delegator receives their full undelegateAmount at completion.      This is an intentional design decision — delegators who have committed to exit      should not bear slash risk for validator misbehavior after their exit request.

params.amount: The amount of RLS to undelegate

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.recipient: The address to receive commission (address(0) to reset to self)
The address to receive rewards (address(0) to reset to self)
params.validatorAddress: The validator whose pool to set the recipient for

setWhitelistRoot(bytes32).details: Admin-only. Enables the whitelist as a side effect — a new root      is always a commitment to gate delegations. Does not invalidate      previously cached `isWhitelistVerified` entries.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

updateCommission(uint256).details: Increases are queued with a delay; decreases apply immediately and cancel any pending increase

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

activatePendingCommission().notice: Activate a pending commission increase after the delay period

applyPoolSlash(address,uint256).notice: Slashes delegated stake proportionally and transfers slashed tokens to ConsensusRegistry

claimDelegationRewards(address).notice: Claim accumulated delegation rewards from a validator's pool

completeUndelegation(address).notice: Complete undelegation after the unbonding period has elapsed

delegate(address,uint256).notice: Delegate ERC-20 RLS to a validator's pool

delegateWithProof(address,uint256,uint25.notice: Delegate while submitting a Merkle proof of PrelaunchLockbox inclusion

disableWhitelist().notice: Kill-switch: disables the whitelist gate on `delegate`

distributePoolRewards(address,uint256).notice: Distribute ERC-20 RLS rewards to a validator's delegation pool

getCommissionRecipient(address).notice: Get the commission recipient for a validator

getDelegatorPosition(address,address).notice: Get a delegator's full position details

getEffectivePosition(address,address).notice: Get a delegator's effective position after pending slashes

getPendingCommission(address).notice: Get pending commission for a validator

getRewardRecipient(address,address).notice: Get the reward recipient for a delegator in a specific validator's pool

getTotalDelegatedStake(address).notice: Get the total delegated stake for a validator

getValidatorPool(address).notice: Get a validator's pool configuration and state

isWhitelistVerified(address).notice: Whether `account` has already proven Merkle inclusion

poolRegistered(address).notice: Check if a pool is registered for a validator

registerPool(uint256).notice: Validator registers their delegation pool with a commission rate

requestUndelegation(address,uint256).notice: Request undelegation (starts unbonding period based on CURRENT config)

rlsToken().notice: Get the RLS token address (ERC-20 staking token)

setAcceptingDelegations(bool).notice: Validator toggles whether their pool accepts new delegations

setCommissionRecipient(address).notice: Validator sets a custom recipient for commission rewards

setRewardRecipient(address,address).notice: Set a custom recipient for delegation rewards

setWhitelistRoot(bytes32).notice: Set the whitelist Merkle root and enable the gate

whitelistEnabled().notice: Whether the whitelist gate on `delegate` is enabled

whitelistRoot().notice: Current whitelist Merkle root (root of `(address, balance)` leaves from the PrelaunchLockbox snapshot)
